description: Musical requires characters with clearly statable desires, an ensemble that amplifies emotional states, and musical numbers that do traceable narrative work — I Want song, showstopper, and reprise are mandatory.

character_constraints.description: A character with a clearly statable desire — the I Want song must be possible. What they want drives the story.
A social body that amplifies emotional states — chorus, community, or group whose collective energy shapes tone.
The primary source of opposition or desire — the relationship that generates the central dramatic tension.

relationship_constraints.description: What stands between the protagonist and their want — the engine of every musical number.

place_constraints.description: A setting consistent with the song logic — where bursting into song feels natural within the established reality.
A location that accommodates large-scale musical numbers — town square, theater, ballroom, street.

object_constraints.description: An object carried through multiple musical numbers with transforming meaning — rose, letter, hat, instrument.
A physical embodiment of a recurring musical theme — the object the reprise references.

element_rules.description: Every musical number must do traceable narrative work — the situation must change when the song ends.
element_rules.testable_condition: Each musical number in the timeline is followed by a changed relationship, decision, or revelation.

element_rules.description: I Want song, showstopper, and reprise are all mandatory structural beats.
element_rules.testable_condition: The timeline contains an 'I Want' scene in the first quarter, a showstopper in the third quarter, and a reprise near the climax.
